metadata.title: Sparse Parameterization for Epitomic Dataset Distillation
organizations: School of Software Engineering, Xi'an Jiaotong University
Shenzhen Institute of Advanced Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences

sections.title: Dataset distillation
content: Dataset distillation aims to obtain smaller synthetic datasets that capture the essential information of the originals.
The success of dataset distillation relies on two essential factors:
Proper parameterization of the synthetic dataset
Effective design of the matching objective to align it with the original

sections.title: Previous explorations
content: Spatial redundancy exists within individual synthetic images and between different synthetic images.
Naive image-independent parameterization: each learnable parameter basis (synthetic image) is optimized independently
Recent efficient parameterization: either only consider the connections between synthetic images, or do not reduce the redundancy inside the synthetic image thoroughly

sections.title: Sparse parameterization
content: Our sparse parameterization strategy leverages principles from representation learning paradigms, including convolutional neural network, vision transformer, dictionary learning, and sparse coding.
Spatial-agnostic epitomic tokens (SAETs) E are shared among all patches in all synthetic images, reducing spatial redundancy
Sparse coding matrices (SCMs) Aiare stored in a sparse manner, eliminating inefficient parameters and bringing better generalization
Feature-recurrent network (FReeNet) Φθrecurrently use the sparsely combined features, alleviating storage burden

figures.description: Proposed SPEED framework overview showing SCMs, SAETs, and FReeNet architecture for dataset matching.
